title: Anthropic ends included Fable 5 access tonight after three deadline extensions
subtitle: Starting tomorrow, Max and Team Premium keep Fable 5 at 50% of weekly limits, Pro and Team Standard move to $10/$50 per million tokens — the model has been re-priced three times in six weeks.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic's third extension of free Claude Fable 5 access expires at 11:59 PM Pacific tonight. Under the new plan structure, effective July 20, Max and Team Premium subscribers get Fable 5 as a permanent feature at 50% of their standard weekly usage limits, while Pro and Team Standard subscribers get a one-time $100 usage credit and then must pay API rates of $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens. Anthropic emailed the change to subscribers on July 13, three months after Fable 5's June release.

This is the second re-shuffle since Fable 5 shipped. The model was launched, then paused by an export-control review that Anthropic and the Trump administration resolved on July 1, then reopened to Pro, Max, and Team subscribers under a temporary free window that was originally supposed to close July 7. It got pushed to July 12, then to July 19, and is now ending on that date with a permanent price boundary between plan tiers. Fable 5's availability terms have effectively been rewritten three times since June 10.

The re-pricing lands into a specific competitive moment. OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna went fully public on July 9 with a $1 to $5 per million input token band and a 1 million token context window, and Anthropic's own subscriber emails cite 'stronger-than-expected demand' as the reason limits are being tightened. The pattern that is emerging — top-tier plans keep frontier access, mid-tier plans get pushed to metered billing — is the same one Google took with Gemini Ultra earlier this year and the one OpenAI's Business tier introduced on July 1. The consumer flat-rate model for frontier reasoning is quietly disappearing.

For learners: read the plan terms, not the marketing copy. In mid-2026, the difference between 'included' and 'metered' access to a frontier model is often the difference between building a hobby project and burning $500 a month on tokens without realizing it. Before you commit to a workflow that assumes constant access to a specific model, check the price sheet, check the usage limits by tier, and check the vendor's track record of changing them. Anthropic has now changed Fable 5's terms three times in six weeks — that is the current normal, not the exception.

sources.title: Anthropic Moves Claude Fable 5 to Pay-as-You-Go Pricing From July 20 — International Business Times
Claude Fable 5 Ends Subscription Limbo: Permanent for Max, Credits-Only for Pro — Tech Times
Anthropic slashes Claude Fable 5 limits in Max and Team Premium and pushes Pro users toward API pricing — The Decoder
